summary: Israel's defence minister announced unrestricted IDF operations in Lebanon, citing heavy strikes that eliminated many Hezbollah militants and damaged terror infrastructure. A Hezbollah rocket and drone attack killed an IDF soldier and injured 13 in Kfar Tebnit, prompting Israeli retaliation on Hezbollah sites. Iran's delegation protested US threats and discussed a memorandum with the US, Qatar and Pakistan, while US President Trump warned Iran over Hezbollah and Lebanon.
key_points: Israel's defence minister said there are no limits on IDF actions in Lebanon and that many Hezbollah terrorists and terror infrastructure have been destroyed (Source: @pkpoi/142903).

A Hezbollah rocket and drone barrage in Kfar Tebnit, southern Lebanon killed Sgt. First Class Nir Ben Ari and injured 13 soldiers, leading to IDF strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure (Source: @manniefabian/58640).
Four IDF soldiers, including Staff Sgt. Liav Kababia and Staff Sgt. Yoav Klein, were killed in a Hezbollah attack on a tank in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/202026).

The IDF chief of staff affirmed the objective in southern Lebanon remains defending northern Israeli communities, with operations focused on the Ali Taher and Beaufort areas (Source: @manniefabian/58648).
Hezbollah has built an underground drone base near Majdal Zoun, several hundred meters long and 29 m deep, with Iranian assistance (Source: @manniefabian/58649).

Iranian delegation lodged a protest with the US and is meeting with US, Qatar and Pakistan officials to discuss a memorandum, noting US failure to guarantee a Lebanon ceasefire (Source: @asafroz/50554).
US President Trump warned Iran over its support for Hezbollah and said he could act after a 60‑day period concerning Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/62413).

sections.executive_summary: Israel's defence minister declared no restrictions on IDF operations in Lebanon, reporting heavy strikes that killed many Hezbollah militants and damaged terror infrastructure. A Hezbollah rocket and drone attack in Kfar Tebnit killed an IDF soldier and injured 13, prompting Israeli retaliation on Hezbollah sites. Iran's delegation protested US threats and discussed a memorandum with the US, Qatar and Pakistan, while US President Trump warned Iran over Hezbollah support.

points: Iranian officials protested US verbal threats and are holding a joint meeting with the United States, Qatar and Pakistan to discuss implementation of a memorandum of understanding, noting the US has not secured a ceasefire in Lebanon (Source: @asafroz/50554).
Iranian delegation lodged a formal protest with the US side and is weighing response options to President Trump's recent threats (Source: @presstv/195022).

Defence Minister Yoav Gallant (Katz) stated there are no limits on IDF actions in Lebanon, that many Hezbollah terrorists and terror infrastructure have been eliminated, and that IDF positions remain on the "yellow line" after the ceasefire announcement (Source: @pkpoi/142903).

IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir reiterated that the mission in southern Lebanon is to defend northern Israeli communities, focusing on the Ali Taher and Beaufort areas, and warned the ceasefire is fragile requiring high readiness (Source: @manniefabian/58648).

Four IDF soldiers, including Staff Sgt. Liav Kababia and Staff Sgt. Yoav Klein, were killed in a Hezbollah attack on a tank in southern Lebanon; a fifth soldier's name is pending release (Source: @New_security8200/202026; @manniefabian/58643).
A pre‑planned military drill is underway in the Jaffa area, with increased security force movement and construction work expected to conclude on Tuesday morning (Source: @manniefabian/58646).

Two Hamas financial operatives, Hussein Qadra and Mohammed Farra, were killed in an Israeli airstrike in southern Gaza; they had overseen a network transferring over half a billion NIS to Hamas's military wing (Source: @manniefabian/58645).

Hezbollah launched a rocket and drone barrage on an IDF position in Kfar Tebnit, southern Lebanon, killing Sgt. First Class Nir Ben Ari and wounding 13 troops; Israel responded with strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in the Nabatieh area (Source: @manniefabian/58640).

Hezbollah has constructed an underground drone "airbase" near Majdal Zoun, several hundred meters long and 29 m deep, built with direct Iranian assistance and capable of housing standard vehicles (Source: @manniefabian/58649).
Israeli forces reported killing a large number of Hezbollah militants and damaging multiple terror infrastructure sites following recent hostilities (Source: @pkpoi/142903).

key_developments: Israel's defence minister announced unrestricted IDF operations in Lebanon, claiming heavy strikes eliminated many Hezbollah militants and destroyed terror infrastructure (Source: @pkpoi/142903).
Hezbollah's rocket and drone attack in Kfar Tebnit killed an IDF soldier and injured 13, leading to Israeli retaliation on Hezbollah sites (Source: @manniefabian/58640).
Four IDF soldiers, including Liav Kababia and Yoav Klein, were killed in a Hezbollah tank attack in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/202026).
Hezbollah's underground drone base near Majdal Zoun, built with Iranian assistance, spans several hundred meters and reaches 29 m depth (Source: @manniefabian/58649).

Iranian delegation protested US threats and is meeting with US, Qatar and Pakistan officials to discuss a memorandum, highlighting US failure to guarantee a Lebanon ceasefire (Source: @asafroz/50554).
US President Trump warned Iran over its support for Hezbollah and indicated possible action after a 60‑day period concerning Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/62413).
Two Hamas financial operatives were killed in a Gaza airstrike, disrupting a network that moved over half a billion NIS to Hamas's military wing (Source: @manniefabian/58645).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to possess the capability to launch coordinated rocket and drone attacks against IDF positions in southern Lebanon, as demonstrated in Kfar Tebnit, and maintains underground infrastructure for UAV operations with Iranian support. Iranian involvement deepens the threat landscape, providing technical assistance and funding to Hezbollah's drone base. Israeli retaliation shows a willingness to conduct targeted strikes, but the fragile ceasefire and high readiness posture indicate a risk of rapid escalation.
sections.regional_response: Iran is actively engaging diplomatically, protesting US verbal threats and seeking to solidify a memorandum with the US, Qatar and Pakistan, while emphasizing the need for a ceasefire in Lebanon. The United States, through President Trump's statements, is applying pressure on Iran and Hezbollah, signaling potential punitive actions if the situation does not improve. Israel maintains a robust defensive stance, reinforcing positions along the border and conducting pre‑planned drills to demonstrate operational readiness.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah's underground drone facilities near Majdal Zoun should be intensified, given their Iranian backing and potential to launch UAV attacks. Continued surveillance of cross‑border rocket and drone launches is essential to anticipate future IDF casualties. Diplomatic channels involving Iran, the US, Qatar and Pakistan may influence the ceasefire dynamics in Lebanon and should be tracked for shifts in policy or escalation triggers.
